zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Peart於1615年8月26日出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾阿爾斯同時，她的父親Richard Peart28歲，母親Susanna George25歲。她於1631年9月29日與William Mumford在英國格盧斯特斯爾阿爾斯同結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1642年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加羅德 易曼德紐波特紐波特享年27歲。

nameMeanings.name: Peart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Perth (Perths), which is recorded as Pertc. 1128. The placename derives from Pictish perta ‘(at the) thicket, copse’.
English: nickname, probably from a shortened form of Middle English, Old French apert ‘open, plain to see’, of persons ‘frank, honest’ or ‘accomplished’.

variants.name: Pert
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Peart  .

variants.name: Peard
meanings: English (Devon): perhaps a variant of Peart with voicing of final -d.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: Providence Founded
historicEvents.description: In 1636 Providence is founded as a settlement for Rhode Island by Roger Williams

historicEvents.title: First Baptist Church
historicEvents.description: Roger Williams establishes the First Baptist Church of Rhode Island in Providence during 1638.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Peart was born on 26 August 1615, in Alstone, Gloucestershire, England, her father, Richard Peart, was 28 and her mother, Susanna George, was 25. She married William Mumford on 29 September 1631, in Alstone, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1642, in Newport, Newport, Rhode Island, British Colonial America, at the age of 27.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Peart(이)가 1615년 8월 26일 영국 그루스스터실르 알스톤에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Peart(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Susanna George(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1631년 9월 29일 영국 그루스스터실르 알스톤에서 William Mumford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1642년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 호드 이슬랜드 뉴포트 뉴포트에서 27세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Peart родилась 26 августа 1615 г. в местности алстоун, глоусестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Peart было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Susanna George, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Mumford 29 сентября 1631 г. в местности алстоун, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1642 г. в местности нюпорт, нюпорт, род исланд, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 27 лет.
